evals.prompt: I have a Java library I've been working on. It's a Maven project with groupId com.github.jsmith and artifactId json-utils, version 1.0.0. I want to publish it to Maven Central so other people can use it as a dependency. I've never done this before — where do I start? My GitHub username is jsmith and the repo is at github.com/jsmith/json-utils. The library is Apache 2.0 licensed.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive step-by-step guide covering namespace verification, GPG setup, portal token generation, complete pom.xml additions, settings.xml configuration, and the deploy command. MUST catch the com.github groupId mistake and correct to io.github.

assertions.text: Corrects com.github.jsmith to io.github.jsmith and explains why
Mentions namespace verification on central.sonatype.com
Includes GPG key generation and key server upload commands
Shows central-publishing-maven-plugin XML configuration
Shows maven-source-plugin configuration
Shows maven-javadoc-plugin configuration
Shows maven-gpg-plugin configuration
POM metadata includes name, description, url, license, developer, and scm
Uses the user's actual project coordinates (jsmith, json-utils) not generic placeholders

evals.prompt: I want to publish my Kotlin library to Maven Central from Gradle without using any third-party publishing plugins. I want full control and don't want dependencies on community plugins that might break. My groupId is io.github.devuser, artifactId is krypto-utils, version 2.0.0. The project is at github.com/devuser/krypto-utils. Can you set up the complete build.gradle.kts?
evals.expected_output: Complete build.gradle.kts using only Gradle built-ins (maven-publish, signing) plus custom tasks for checksum generation, zip bundling, Portal API upload, and status polling. Should include Dokka for Kotlin javadoc.

assertions.text: Uses maven-publish and signing plugins (Gradle built-ins), not community publishing plugins
Generates .md5 and .sha1 checksums explicitly (not relying on a plugin)
Bundles artifacts into a zip file following Maven repository layout
Uploads via HTTP to central.sonatype.com/api/v1/publisher/upload
Polls deployment status via central.sonatype.com/api/v1/publisher/status
Mentions Dokka for Kotlin documentation JAR generation
Includes GPG signing configuration
POM metadata includes all required elements (name, description, url, licenses, developers, scm)
Uses the user's actual coordinates (io.github.devuser, krypto-utils, 2.0.0)

evals.expected_output: Should present multiple migration options (update URLs, switch to vanniktech, custom integration) with pros/cons. Must include the correct OSSRH staging API URL and note credential changes.

assertions.text: Includes the OSSRH staging API URL: ossrh-staging-api.central.sonatype.com
Includes the new snapshot URL: central.sonatype.com/repository/maven-snapshots/
Mentions that old OSSRH credentials won't work — new Central Portal tokens are required
Presents at least 2 options (update existing plugin config OR switch to different plugin/approach)
Shows updated build.gradle.kts code for at least one option

evals.prompt:
My mvn deploy succeeded in uploading but the deployment failed validation on central.sonatype.com. The errors say 'Missing: no javadoc jar found' and 'Invalid POM: missing required element: scm'. Also I'm getting a 401 Unauthorized when I try to re-deploy. I generated my credentials on oss.sonatype.org months ago. Here's my current pom.xml build plugins section:

<build>
    <plugins>
        <plugin>
            <groupId>org.sonatype.central</groupId>
            <artifactId>central-publishing-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            <version>0.9.0</version>
            <extensions>true</extensions>
            <configuration>
                <publishingServerId>central</publishingServerId>
            </configuration>
        </plugin>
        <plugin>
            <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
            <artifactId>maven-source-plugin</artifactId>
            <version>3.3.1</version>
            <executions>
                <execution>
                    <id>attach-sources</id>
                    <goals><goal>jar-no-fork</goal></goals>
                </execution>
            </executions>
        </plugin>
        <plugin>
            <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
            <artifactId>maven-gpg-plugin</artifactId>
            <version>3.2.7</version>
            <executions>
                <execution>
                    <id>sign-artifacts</id>
                    <phase>verify</phase>
                    <goals><goal>sign</goal></goals>
                </execution>
            </executions>
        </plugin>
    </plugins>
</build>
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses all three issues: missing javadoc plugin, missing SCM element, and stale OSSRH credentials. Provides actionable XML fixes and explains the credential migration.

assertions.text: Identifies the missing maven-javadoc-plugin and provides its XML configuration
Identifies the missing <scm> POM element and provides an <scm> XML block
Explains that old oss.sonatype.org credentials don't work with Central Portal
Directs user to generate new tokens at central.sonatype.com/usertoken
Fixes are concrete XML blocks, not just descriptions
